Current_Implementation: PuLP binary knapsack (allocate_procurement_budget): maximize sum(SRRS) s.t. sum(Investment) <= Rs 1 crore; affordability filter; two-stage Safety Reserve floor for S1/S2
Gap_Relative_To_STEP35: None for single-budget single-unit case. Budget value is a single config constant (PROCUREMENT_BUDGET); not parameterized per run/scenario in the solver
Recommendation: Expose budget as a runtime argument / scenario list rather than a fixed config constant

Current_Implementation: Produces a ranked procurement PORTFOLIO (which items to fund now, ordered by SRRS / priority class) in railway_procurement_plan.csv
Gap_Relative_To_STEP35: No MULTI-PERIOD / time-phased schedule. All outputs are a single-period snapshot; no sequencing across months/quarters, no phased budget release, no lead-time-aware ordering of when to place POs
Recommendation: Add a multi-period model (periods x items) with per-period budget and lead-time-aware delivery timing to produce a true procurement SEQUENCE

Current_Implementation: SRRS_Rank, Procurement_Priority_Class, Service_Risk_Priority_Class, SRRS_Per_Rupee, Funding_Driver; executive_top10_procurement.csv; railway_funding_explainability.csv (funded vs rejected with driver attribution)
Gap_Relative_To_STEP35: None. This requirement is fully met and production-grade with explainability
Recommendation: No action needed; capability is mature

Current_Implementation: Single-budget knapsack allocation + two-stage Safety Reserve + budget-utilization reporting; each of 6 BUs runs its own independent allocation
Gap_Relative_To_STEP35: No OPTIMAL division of a SHARED enterprise budget ACROSS the 6 business units (enterprise_rollup only CONCATENATES per-BU outputs; each BU gets its own fixed Rs 1 crore). No nested / hierarchical capital allocation
Recommendation: Add an enterprise-level allocation that distributes one shared budget across BUs to maximize total enterprise SRRS (nested knapsack or shared-budget LP)

Current_Implementation: build_budget_scenario reports utilization at a single fixed Rs 1 crore; management_reports narrates 'four budget scenarios' from pre-exported page8 data
Gap_Relative_To_STEP35: Scenarios are REPORTED at discrete fixed levels, not SOLVED as a marginal-return / efficient-frontier curve. No automatic sweep of budget levels through the solver
Recommendation: Run the knapsack across a sweep of budget levels to emit a marginal-coverage-per-rupee frontier

Current_Implementation: Strategic stock distributed across BUs by deterministic depot->BU column mapping (railway_strategic_allocation.py)
Gap_Relative_To_STEP35: Strategic allocation is a fixed mapping, not an optimization; cannot rebalance strategic reserves to where service-risk is highest
Recommendation: Optional: replace deterministic mapping with a service-risk-weighted allocation if rebalancing is in scope for STEP35

Current_Implementation: Repo docs (remote_migration_plan.md, STEP33/34 reports) already define STEP35 = git push / remote migration to PRIVATE uppukarthik-code repo
Gap_Relative_To_STEP35: An OPTIMIZATION STEP35 would collide with the documented release STEP35. No optimization-flavored STEP35 spec exists anywhere in the repo
Recommendation: Rename the optimization initiative (e.g. STEP35-OPT or a distinct step number) to avoid colliding with the documented release/push STEP35
